news.headline: Khurana from Wellington discusses global fixed income on Bloomberg, focusing on macro trends.
Shelby County Federal Credit Union appoints Raffael Crockett as CEO to boost growth and financial stability.
news.summary: In Memphis, Tennessee on February 13, 2025, Shelby County Federal Credit Union announced the appointment of Raffael (Ralph) Crockett as the new Chief Executive Officer. Crockett, who brings over thirty years of credit union experience, is set to lead the organization towards greater financial stability and success. This move marks a significant shift in leadership for the credit union as it looks towards a promising future under Crockett's guidance.

news.headline: Global acne drugs market expected to reach USD 4.59 billion by 2030, with a market value of USD 3.61 billion in 2023.
news.summary: The market for acne drugs is divided by type into injection acne drugs and external acne drugs, with applications targeted towards teenagers and adults. In Bangalore, India, as of February 13, 2025, the global acne drugs market was valued at approximately USD 3616.6 million in 2023. It is projected to reach a revised size in the future, indicating a significant growth trend in the industry.

news.headline: Endeavour Mining received a notification from Blackrock Inc. about its interests in the company as of 12 Feb 2025.
news.summary: Endeavour Mining plc has been notified by Blackrock, Inc. on 13 February 2025 about their interests in the Company as of 12 February 2025. This notification pertains to the ownership stake or shares held by Blackrock, Inc. in the Company, indicating the extent of their investment in the mining firm. The disclosure is important for stakeholders and investors to understand the shareholding pattern and potential influence of a major financial player like Blackrock, Inc. on the operations and decisions of Endeavour Mining plc. The specific numbers or percentage of shares held by Blackrock, Inc. have not been disclosed in the announcement.

news.headline: Toll Brothers opens new luxury home community, Hearthfield, with 54 home sites in Collegeville, PA; Sales Center now open. #RealEstate #LuxuryHomes
news.summary: In Collegeville, Pennsylvania, Toll Brothers, Inc. (NYSE:TOL), the nation’s top luxury home builder, revealed the launch of its latest community, Hearthfield. Situated in Collegeville, Hearthfield comprises 54 single-family home sites, uniquely designed with each home positioned on a cul-de-sac. The Sales Center and model home can be visited at 304 Grayson Court in Collegeville.

news.headline: Outrig acquires 2 RV storage facilities in California, expanding its portfolio to 14 properties nationwide.
news.summary: In Oakley, California, on February 13, 2025, Outrig has increased its national storage holdings from 12 to 14 properties. This expansion came through the purchase of two top-tier RV storage facilities in California. The acquisitions include Oakley Executive RV and Boat Storage located in the Bay Area, and All Valleys RV Storage. This move signifies a significant growth for Outrig in the storage sector, boosting their presence in the region.

news.headline: Penske Automotive Group beats Q4 earnings with $3.54 EPS, $7.72B sales (up 6% YoY), 3% increase in car deliveries.
news.summary: Penske Automotive Group, Inc. (NYSE:PAG) saw its shares rise on Thursday following its recent report. In the fourth quarter, the company achieved adjusted earnings per share of $3.54, surpassing expectations of $3.29 per share. Quarterly sales totaled $7.72 billion, marking a 6% increase from the previous year and exceeding analyst estimates of $7.58 billion. The delivery of new and used retail automotive units rose by 3%, while retail automotive same-store revenue experienced a 5% growth. New vehicle sales were up by 7%, used vehicle sales increased by 1%, finance and insurance saw a 3% decline, and service and parts revenue also rose.

news.headline: Trump memo on reciprocal tariffs to mention Brazil ethanol tariffs - Fox News.
Global environmental sensing market collects data on air, water, soil, weather and climate to aid sustainability.
news.summary: "Data from environmental sensing and monitoring technologies, which utilize sensors and satellites to collect information on air, water, soil, weather, and climate, offer real-time insights for tracking pollution, natural disasters, and ecosystem health. These technologies play a crucial role in supporting sustainability, public health, and climate action efforts."

news.headline: HR Advisory Firm McLean & Company emphasizes HR's role in maximizing social media benefits for organizations through better use of technology.
news.summary: A recent update from McLean & Company, a leading global HR research and advisory firm, emphasizes the significance of maximizing technology utilization, particularly through social media, to effectively achieve organizational objectives. The expertise of HR professionals plays a crucial role in harnessing the power of social media to optimize performance and meet key business priorities.

news.headline: Moleculin Biotech raises $5.8M through warrant exercise. Late-stage pharma firm targeting tumors and viruses.
news.summary: Houston, Texas - On February 13, 2025, Moleculin Biotech, Inc. (Nasdaq: MBRX) disclosed partnerships, positioning it as a leading pharmaceutical entity specializing in challenging tumor and virus treatments. The Company's recent agreements exhibit its commitment to advancing therapeutic options for complex medical conditions.

news.headline: SQDC's Q3 fiscal 2024-2025: Comprehensive income $40.5 million, tax revenues $72.6 million, remittance to govts $113.1 million.
news.summary: In the third quarter of the fiscal year ending on January 4, 2025, the Société québécoise du cannabis (SQDC) recorded comprehensive income of $40.5 million, compared to $33.0 million in the same period the previous year. Tax revenues from consumer and excise taxes generated by SQDC's operations reached $72.6 million, with $51.2 million directed to the provincial government and $21.1 million to the federal government. In total, SQDC will remit $113.1 million to both levels of government, with $92.0 million allocated to the Quebec treasury for reinvestment, primarily in cannabis-related prevention efforts and research. Overall sales for the quarter amounted to $235.9 million, showing an increase from $201.6 million.

news.headline: Zebra Tech's Q4 earnings show revenue surged 32% to $1.33 billion, exceeding estimates, CEO credits strong retail demand.
news.summary: Zebra Technologies Corp (NASDAQ:ZBRA) recently announced their Q4 financial results. The mobile computing company reported a significant sales increase of 32.2% to $1.33 billion, surpassing analyst predictions of $1.32 billion. Additionally, Zebra disclosed adjusted earnings of $4.00 per share, which exceeded expectations of $3.94 per share. Despite the positive results, the stock price experienced a decline post-report. Both of Zebra's main business segments, namely Enterprise Visibility & Mobility, also demonstrated substantial growth during this period. For further details, you can access the complete story on Benzinga.com.

Cornerstone Research promotes nine senior staff to VP and principal roles, strengthening expertise in economic consulting.

This decision spans various practices and industries within the company. The announcement was made in San Francisco on February 13, 2025.

news.headline: Tofu secures $12M Series A funding from SignalFire and others for martech platform targeting enterprise GTM teams.

SignalFire spearheaded the investment round, joined by other key contributors including HubSpot Ventures, Tau Ventures, Correlation Ventures, Alumni.

news.headline: Regal Cineworld Group appoints Adam Rymer as Chief Commercial Officer – US. Rymer brings 20+ years of experience.
news.summary: In Knoxville, Tennessee on February 13, 2025, Regal Cineworld Group, a prominent worldwide theater operator, unveiled the selection of Adam Rymer as the inaugural Chief Commercial Officer for the US. Rymer, a highly regarded C-level executive with over 20 years of experience, is set to oversee commercial operations for the company in the US.

news.headline: Soter Insure secures Series A funding led by Galaxy to enhance digital asset insurance solutions. ABU DHABI, UAE, Feb. 13, 2025.
news.summary: Various prominent companies, including Brevan Howard Digital, Karatage, Token Bay, and Pharsalus, have teamed up to support Soter's initiative in offering specialized insurance solutions for the digital asset industry. The collaboration aims to address the growing demand for innovative insurance products within the sector. The partnership was announced in Abu Dhabi, UAE on February 13, 2025. Soter Insure, the insurance provider spearheading the mission, aims to cater to the unique needs of the digital asset market and ensure comprehensive coverage for businesses operating in this space.

news.headline: DoubleLine Research compares Microsoft Corp. debt to U.S. Treasuries in new report by Portfolio Manager Mariya Entina.
news.summary: In Tampa, Florida on February 13, 2025, Portfolio Manager Mariya Entina from DoubleLine Capital released a research paper comparing the creditworthiness and future prospects of bonds from Microsoft Corp. to those issued by the U.S. government. The study, conducted by CFA® and CPA, evaluated key financial metrics to analyze the bonds' performance, shedding light on important figures and trends within the investment-grade bond market.

news.headline: The Charlie Cart Project launches podcast celebrating community wins around food; marks 10th anniversary.
news.summary: Celebrating its 10th anniversary, The Charlie Cart Project (TCCP) based in Berkeley, California, has introduced a podcast to commemorate the occasion. The podcast, titled #CharlieCartPodcast, features inspiring tales of food heroes impacting children and future generations through diverse food education methods. Hosted by renowned documentarian Kelly Duane de la Vega and executive produced by TCCP’s executive director Carolyn Federman, The Charlie Cart Podcast showcases these initiatives. The project aims to shed light on the importance of food education for kids.

news.headline: Tim Ryan of Citi joins Battelle's Board of Directors, bringing his tech expertise. Joined in 2024, he has driven firm's tech and legacy teams' processes.
news.summary: COLUMBUS, Ohio - Battelle made public the addition of Tim Ryan to its Board of Directors. Ryan, who currently holds the position of Head of Technology and Business Enablement at Citi, officially joined the board. His arrival took effect immediately. Ryan's tenure at Citi since June 2024 has seen him lead the Technology and Legacy Franchise divisions. He has been instrumental in streamlining and updating processes across the organization. In addition to his board role, Ryan is a key member of Citi's Executive Management Team. Prior to his current role, Ryan held notable leadership positions, including a two-term appointment.

news.headline: Treasury WI 30y yield 4.736% before $25 billion auction.
FOX Nation to premiere "The Tides That Bind: Inside Alabama Football" on Feb 19, following team post Nick Saban.
news.summary: A brand-new docuseries titled "The Tides That Bind: Inside Alabama" is set to premiere on FOX Nation on February 19th. This series will provide an inside look at the University of Alabama's football team as they navigate their first season following the departure of renowned head coach Nick Saban. The documentary will offer multiple parts and is anticipated to offer an exclusive behind-the-scenes perspective on the team's journey.

news.headline: Cherry Bekaert report on 2024 PE market shows renewed optimism for M&A with key developments. Optimism for 2025 outlook.
news.summary: In Raleigh, North Carolina, on February 13, 2025, Cherry Bekaert (referred to as "the Firm") published its yearly year-end Private Equity Industry Report, focusing on key occurrences that influenced the private investment sector in 2024. The report showcases a resurgence of positivity within the private equity (PE) domain.

news.headline: FBI prevented $285M loss from crypto scams targeting romance and “pig butchering” victims in US.
news.summary: The US Department of Justice has taken action in response to cases involving victims of romance and "pig butchering" scams related to cryptocurrency. The department's intervention came after identifying individuals who had fallen victim to these schemes.

news.headline: O-RAN ALLIANCE plans Global PlugFest Spring 2025 with 23 hosts at 18 labs worldwide to accelerate O-RAN products.
news.summary: The O-RAN ALLIANCE has announced the O-RAN Global PlugFest Spring 2025, which will be hosted by 23 hosts in 18 labs worldwide. The alliance is inviting all of its Members and Participants to participate in the event, offering the opportunity for well-coordinated testing and integration in a collaborative and neutral environment.

news.headline: Noël sees brighter outlook for US than Europe in latest report on economic prospects.
Gabelli Global Small & Mid Cap Value Trust declares $0.16 per share Q1 distribution; payout on March 24, 2025 to shareholders.
news.summary: On February 13, 2025, in Rye, NY, the Gabelli Global Small and Mid Cap Value Trust's Board of Trustees announced a cash distribution of $0.16 per share for the Fund with the stock symbol GGZ. This distribution will be paid on March 24, 2025, to shareholders of record as of March 17, 2025.

news.headline: West Pharma (NYSE:WST) beats Q4 EPS at $1.82, sales at $748.8M, surpassing consensus. Company have mixed organic net sales growth.
news.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. reported a strong fourth quarter with an adjusted EPS of $1.82, surpassing the expected $1.72. The company's sales totaled $748.8 million, exceeding the expected $740.47 million. The Proprietary Products Segment's sales grew by 3.4% to $613.9 million, with organic net sales up by 4.5%. High-value products accounted for 74% of the segment net sales, driven by customer demand for self-injection device platforms. The Biologics market unit experienced high-single-digit organic net sales growth, while the Pharma market unit saw mid-single-digit organic net sales growth. The fourth-quarter sales included a $25 million benefit from customer incentives earned. Overall, the company performed well with strong sales and earnings.

news.headline: USL launches Division One men's league, aims to elevate sport by expanding opportunities, aligning globally. TAMPA, Fla., Feb 13, 2025.
news.summary: Expanding access to elite soccer in various communities, boosting the sport, and moving closer to international standards, the United Soccer League (USL) revealed in Tampa, Fla., on Feb. 13, 2025 that it will be introducing new opportunities for teams, players, and fans.

news.headline: OAC celebrates 20th Anniversary, empowering individuals affected by obesity through advocacy and support since 2005.
news.summary: In Tampa, Florida, on February 13, 2025, the Obesity Action Coalition (OAC) commemorates its 20th Anniversary. Since its establishment in 2005 by a small group, the organization has been dedicated to supporting individuals impacted by obesity. Over the past 20 years, OAC has focused on advocacy, awareness, education, and providing assistance to those affected by the disease. This milestone signifies two decades of commitment to addressing obesity-related issues and helping individuals, with significant progress made in raising awareness and providing support.

news.headline: Amped Fitness® opened in Murfreesboro, offering energizing experience for members at 1720 Old Fort Parkway.
news.summary: On February 13, 2025, in Murfreesboro, Tennessee, Amped Fitness® Murfreesboro was launched with great enthusiasm. The gym, situated at 1720 Old Fort Parkway, welcomed its initial members, marking the official beginning of what promises to be a vibrant fitness community. This establishment signifies more than just a place to work out; it represents a movement towards health and wellness.

news.headline: Moody's projects high single-digit revenue increase in 2025, with Q4 revenue climbing 13%. Revenue for the quarter was $1.67 billion.
news.summary: Shares of Moody’s Corp (NYSE:MCO) rose following the release of its fourth-quarter financial results. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of $2.61 for the quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations of $2.59. Quarterly revenue totaled $1.67 billion, reflecting a 13% increase compared to the previous year. However, this figure fell short of the projected $1.7 billion. Moody’s Investors Service (MIS) also saw growth in revenue. You can find the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Agios Pharmaceuticals' study of mitapivat in kids aged 1 to <18 with PK deficiency achieves primary endpoint of hemoglobin response.
news.summary: Agios Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ:AGIO) revealed on Thursday that its ACTIVATE-Kids Phase 3 study of mitapivat in children aged 1 to under 18 years with PK deficiency who are not regularly transfused met its primary endpoint of hemoglobin response. PK deficiency is an uncommon genetic condition impacting red blood cells. The safety findings aligned with the drug's known safety profile for adult patients with PK deficiency who do not undergo regular transfusions. The study's main goal was a hemoglobin response, characterized by a minimum 1.5 g/dL rise in hemoglobin concentration from baseline sustained at two or more scheduled assessments at Weeks 12, 16, and 20. Additional analysis was carried out to compare the hemoglobin response rate for mitapivat with traditional methodology.

AI agents now coach gamers through Balance.fun, using an Ethereum token about to launch. Great news for gamers!
news.summary: With the introduction of the Balance.fun companion platform, AI agents are now able to guide individuals through their video games. This innovative platform is scheduled to release its Ethereum token in the near future.

news.headline: Atria Health expands nationwide to support cardiology practices, offering capital and expertise for growth in value-based care.
news.summary: Atria Health has formed partnerships with cardiologists to establish independent practices, offering financial support, specialized knowledge, and advanced solutions to expand and succeed in the value-based care landscape. The company has teamed up with a prominent cardiology group in Pennsylvania to introduce an ambulatory surgical center, indicating a strategic move towards enhancing healthcare services. This collaboration showcases Atria Health's commitment to fostering growth and development, benefiting both the medical professionals and the community.

news.headline: Sanofi and Johnson & Johnson discontinue late-stage study of vaccine for extraintestinal E. coli after review.
news.summary: Sanofi SA (NASDAQ:SNY) and Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) have halted the E.mbrace phase 3 study assessing a vaccine candidate for extraintestinal pathogenic E. coli. The study was a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter trial aiming to evaluate the effectiveness, safety, and immunogenicity of a single vaccine dose versus a placebo in preventing IED, encompassing sepsis and bacteremia. An independent data monitoring committee conducted a routine review of the trial. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Vaccine skeptic Robert F. Kennedy confirmed as Trump's health secretary by Senate 52-48 vote. Many agencies under his oversight.
news.summary: In a recent vote, the U.S. Senate decisively approved Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as the new head of the Department of Health and Human Services, with a final tally of 52 to 48 in favor. Notably, Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell was the lone Republican to oppose the nomination, while all Democrats voted against it. Holding the position of health secretary, Kennedy will be responsible for leading key health agencies such as the Food and Drug Administration, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, and the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services. He steps into this role at a challenging time, with healthcare expenses on the rise and concerns over the spread of bird flu among wild birds and poultry farms. Additional insights can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Equinix misses Q4 earnings; FFO at $7.92 vs estimate $8.09, revenue $2.26B vs $2.27B. Raises dividend to $4.69. Expects Q1 revenue $2.191B-$2.231B, 2025 revenue $9.033B-$9.133B. Oppenheimer raises price forecast to $975.
news.summary: Equinix, Inc. (NASDAQ: EQIX) recently reported fourth-quarter earnings, with funds from operations (FFO) at $7.92, falling short of the expected $8.09, and revenue at $2.26 billion, slightly below the anticipated $2.27 billion. In response, the Board of Directors decided to raise the quarterly cash dividend by 10% to $4.69 per share on common stock. The company forecasts first-quarter revenue ranging between $2.191 billion to $2.231 billion, differing from the estimated $2.285 billion, and predicts revenue for 2025 to be between $9.033 billion and $9.133 billion, alongside an AFFO per share of $36.69 to $37.51, contrasting the projected $9.404 billion estimate. Oppenheimer analyst Timothy Horan adjusted the price target from $950 to $975, indicating an Outperform rating. Horan highlighted that although initial guidance seemed weak, after accounting for various factors, the results were in line with expectations at 7%-8%.

news.headline: Brigade advises on selecting a telematics provider for fleet safety with focus on customer needs in ever-evolving industry.
news.summary: In Portland, Indiana on February 13, 2025, it was emphasized that selecting the appropriate telematics provider for commercial fleet safety solutions is a significant choice. Brigade delves into the key factors that customers must contemplate to guarantee optimal outcomes. AppLovin surged by 38% and Tyler saw a significant breakout, propelling the indexes higher. Dutch Bros stock soars after 'stand-out quarter', revenue at $342.8M surpassing estimates, 35% YOY increase, $1.56B to $1.58B revenue guidance in 2025. Analysts raise price targets to $100.
news.summary: Dutch Bros Inc (NYSE:BROS) saw a rise in its stock price as it reported strong financial performance for Q4. The company exceeded analyst projections with quarterly revenue of $342.8 million compared to expected $317.8 million, and adjusted earnings of 7 cents per share beating estimates of 2 cents per share. Q4 revenue saw a 35% increase year-over-year. Dutch Bros anticipates full-year 2025 revenue to range between $1.56 billion to $1.58 billion with same shop sales growth projected at 2% to 4%. Analyst Nick Setyan from Wedbush maintained an Outperform rating on Dutch Bros and increased the price target from $65 to $100 post results, indicating confidence in the company's future performance. Setyan considers the guidance to be on the conservative side and anticipates growth in same shop sales.

news.headline: Nasdaq surges 150 points, Cisco beats earnings with $13.9 billion revenue, stock price up. Market overall up.
news.summary: During midday trading, U.S. stocks were on the rise, with the Nasdaq Composite seeing a significant increase of approximately 150 points on Thursday. The Dow Jones Industrial Average was up by 0.33% at 44,513.97, while the NASDAQ rose by 0.78% to 19,804.09. The S&P 500 also experienced growth, posting a 0.51% increase to reach 6,082.97. In terms of sectors, consumer discretionary shares surged by 1.1%, whereas health care shares only saw a slight increase of 0.1% in Thursday's trading session. In recent news, Cisco Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ:CSCO) announced better-than-expected financial results for the second quarter post Wednesday's closing bell. Cisco reported earnings per share of 94 cents for the quarter, surpassing the analyst consensus of 91 cents. The quarterly revenue stood at $13.9 billion, beating the estimated $13.87 billion. Looking ahead, Cisco anticipates third-quarter EPS to fall between 90 and 92 cents, compared to the consensus estimate of 92 cents, with revenue... (For full story, refer to financial news outlet).

news.headline: Renowned investors Peter Thiel and Paolo Ardoino support  blockchain focused on USDt.
news.summary: Last month, a new report indicated that the global economic growth forecast for this year has been revised down to 5.9% from the previous estimate of 6.0%. This adjustment was primarily due to the ongoing supply chain disruptions, rising inflation rates, and the uneven distribution of COVID-19 vaccines worldwide. The report also highlighted that advanced economies are expected to grow by 4.4% in 2022, while emerging economies are projected to see a growth of 5.2%. Additionally, inflation rates are anticipated to rise by 3.9% in advanced economies and 5.8% in emerging economies. The report emphasized the importance of addressing these challenges to ensure sustainable and equitable global economic recovery.
